response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with strengths in web technologies, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but he has virtually no relevant experience for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerator hardware optimization. The role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, and deep learning workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is low-latency application development in DLang and some C/C++ work at National Instruments, but these fall far short of the hardware-software co-design and silicon-level performance analysis the role requires.
strengths: Low-latency systems experience: built high-throughput micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O, caching, and fibers at Sociomantic
C/C++ and systems-level exposure from National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and his bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis with Linux/JACK audio
Broad technical range across embedded, desktop, web, and AI-agent tooling showing adaptability
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or any silicon-level performance analysis — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or AI accelerator workloads whatsoever
Career trajectory and seniority are in web/backend engineering, not hardware-software co-design or principal IC roles in infrastructure/silicon
